placementIntent: Distribute server and active RPC workers to keep related components reasonably close while avoiding excessive concentration of heavy containers.
researchQuestion: Can a static balanced layout reduce the extremes of full co-location and full separation?

selectorPolicy.rationale: Stable node names are retained for compatibility with the current Kustomize overlays; label-based node-pool selection is the preferred direction for future generated clusters.

serverPlacement.placementNotes: The current server manifest is still node-name compatible; later topology profiles can replace this with a label-based selector.

mappingSemantics.activeWorkerNodeMapByWorkerCount: Application-level worker-count mapping currently defined for W4. The active topology uses four LocalAI RPC workers and requires at least three provider-backed Kubernetes worker nodes.
mappingSemantics.defaultWorkerNodeMap: Default mapping aligned with the active balanced-static W4 Kustomize overlay at infra/k8s/overlays/topology/placement/balanced-static-genai-pb-w4.

requiredFutureTargets: optional lower-application-worker-count balanced static compositions if this placement must be evaluated below W4

compositionSelectionPolicy.selectionRationale: This placement profile defines a W4 static balanced layout over at least three provider-backed worker nodes. Infrastructure worker-node count is a compatibility constraint rather than the primary selector for this profile.

expectedBenefits: keeps at least one worker close to the server
expectedRisks: static balance may not reflect real-time resource pressure
the active worker map requires at least three provider-backed worker nodes
partial co-location remains because the server, localai-rpc-b and localai-rpc-d target genai-pb-worker-02

governance.comparisonPolicy: Placement comparisons must keep infrastructure, model, workload, worker count, protocol, and metric collection fixed unless a cycle profile explicitly declares otherwise.
